trigger: LGEN.L's dividend cover has fallen to 1.1, meaning earnings per share of £0.22 cover the £0.20 dividend by only 10% above the payment — below the critical threshold of 1.3.
why_it_matters: A dividend cover below 1.3 signals that the company is paying out nearly all of its earnings as dividends, leaving very little buffer if profits decline. For a dividend investor, this raises the risk of a future dividend cut, which would reduce income and likely cause the share price to fall.
suggested_actions: Review LGEN.L's latest earnings report and management guidance on dividend policy
Consider reducing position size to limit exposure if the dividend is cut
Monitor next quarterly EPS closely — two consecutive quarters below cover 1.3 warrants exit consideration
